text:
Market today: Mixed signals. Micron's blowout earnings are lifting chip sentiment, but Apple dropping 5% on memory-crunch price hikes is a real headwind for the broader tech trade — your kind of names are caught in that crossfire today.

Your holdings: PLTR is your biggest problem — down 17.65% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast. AVGO is your largest position, down 12.08% with a TRIM flag — size plus loss is a dangerous combo on leverage. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history; even a small move against you hits hard, and margin amplifies every tick. SPAL is a 2x leveraged ETF — decay is real and it's already down 18.53%.

Watch: 1) How AVGO reacts to the Apple memory news — Apple pain can bleed into chip suppliers. 2) Whether PLTR holds above your $85 stop; a close near there demands action.

Bottom line: Protect capital first — your two biggest losers are also your two biggest risks on margin.
